title: User asks about the full lifecycle — from creation to receipt
user_persona: A buyer who wants to understand the end-to-end flow before starting

conversation.user: Walk me through what happens to a PO from the moment I create it to when the goods arrive. What are the stages?

bot_must: describe stages: Draft (PoCrt) → Pending Approval → Authorised (PoApp) → Goods Received (GR module) → Closed
mention optional intermediate journeys: Amend (PoAmnd) re-opens, Edit (PoEdt) for minor fixes, Hold (PoHold) pauses, Short-Close (PoScl) ends early
note that GR is a separate module

conversation.user: Who can authorise it? Can I do it myself if I created it?

bot_must: explain that PoApp is a separate activity from PoCrt — authorisation is a discrete journey
note that whether the creator can self-approve depends on configured authorisation rules / role permissions (this is configured per-tenant, not hard-coded)
mention that PoCrt has a TRN4 task (Create and Approve PO) which combines both — only usable by users with authorisation rights

conversation.user: What if I make a mistake after it's approved?

bot_must: explain PoAmnd (Amend Purchase Order) is the path for changes after approval — opens the PO for modification
note POAMDMAINTRN5 (Amend and Approve) re-approves in one shot if user has rights, otherwise POAMDMAINSBT submits for re-approval
mention that some structural changes (e.g., supplier change) may require PoScl + new PoCrt instead of amend

evaluation_metrics.lifecycle_completeness: stages include Draft, Authorised, GR, Closed at minimum
evaluation_metrics.amend_path_explained: PoAmnd correctly described as post-approval modification path
